TextENGNew: ``What?!@k@vS12/01/180100150. I-I do feel like I heard someone mention something like that, but... they were actually serious?!``
...You know how people make those polite, feel-good suggestions as a social courtesy?
The kind where you just laugh and say, 'Yeah, that'd be nice,' without really meaning it.
Those things are fine precisely because they stay as jokes -- but when they actually become real, that's a whole different story!!

``Yeah, dead serious!@k@vS12/03/180300051. Uncle Yoshirou was singing your praises too!@k@vS12/03/180300052. He said the way you livened things up during yesterday's club game at the toy store was absolutely spectacular!``
``Rena had no idea you could play a game like that or make things so exciting!``
changeReason: Rena speaks in third person in the Japanese ('レナも'); original omits this and uses 'I' while new correctly preserves Rena's characteristic third-person speech pattern.

TextENGNew: ``Just between us, the kids sitting near me were saying they wished they could've been at Keiichi-san's table instead!``
changeReason: New translation adds Keiichi-san's name and specifies the children wished they'd been at his table, matching the Japanese more closely than the original's vaguer version.

TextENGNew: ``...Keiichi is a genius when it comes to making any game look like the most fun in the world.``
Come on, I wasn't out there having fun -- I was pouring every last ounce of energy into winning because I couldn't afford to lose...
But apparently, me going all out like that looked incredibly entertaining to everyone watching.

``It's true -- Kei-chan always brings such energy to our regular club activities.@k@vS12/06/180600002. The Coach was saying the Hinamizawa Fighters would really come alive if Kei-chan joined the team.``
``He certainly was.@k@vS12/22/181400002. At last night's meeting, Dr. Irie had nothing but praise for you, Maebara-kun.``
``Keiichi-kun, you have a far greater power to make people enjoy themselves than you give yourself credit for.``
``...Y-You really think so...?``
``This old man didn't put your name forward just because you're one of my friends.@k@vS12/03/180300054. Kei-chan, you genuinely earned it!``
changeReason: Original 'He did it because he's confident you can do the job' differs from new 'Kei-chan, you genuinely earned it'; the Japanese emphasizes believing Keiichi can fulfill the important role.

TextENGNew: ``I was at the meeting too, and I was surprised that not a single person objected.``
``You have a much better reputation around the village than you realize, Maebara-kun.``
``You're well known in Okinomiya too.@k@vS12/06/180600004. People are still talking about that incredible play you made against the Okinomiya Titans the other day.``
``It truly was something else...@k@vS12/04/180400074. Even the reporters who came for Kameda-kun were blown away!``
changeReason: Honorific change from 'Kameda-san' to 'Kameda-kun'; Japanese uses '亀田さん' (san), so the original is accurate.

TextENGNew: ...Ah, that rigged baseball match with Kameda-kun...
As far as the public was concerned, the Koshien-bound pitcher walked me because my batting eye intimidated him...
changeReason: Japanese '甲子園ピッチャー' specifically means 'Koshien pitcher'; original's generic 'talented pitcher' loses this culturally significant reference.

TextENGNew: Kameda-kun and I have had a strange senpai-kouhai dynamic ever since, but I'll leave that story for another time...
``What do you say, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS12/02/180200030. Rena believes you have the talent for this.@k@vS12/02/180200031. So why not give it a try?``
changeReason: New translation omits the specific mention of the Watanagashi committee role that is present in the Japanese and the original translation.

TextENGNew: Watanagashi is an annual village festival held every June.
It's apparently the biggest event on the village calendar, drawing nearly every resident for a huge, lively celebration.
Naturally, an event that large requires an organizing committee centered on the neighborhood association, and they'd been holding meetings and coordinating preparations for months.
changeReason: New translation adds details about the 'neighborhood association' and 'months' of preparation that are present in the Japanese but omitted in the original.

TextENGNew: The festival was only two weeks away, and here they were asking me -- someone who'd barely just moved here -- to join the committee out of the blue!
``F-First of all, I don't know the first thing about committee work, so I'd be completely useless!``
``Don't let the fancy title scare you!``
``The job we unanimously want you for is -- drumroll please -- emcee for the bargain auction!``
``Wh-What the heck is that?!@k@vS12/01/180100154. Am I supposed to be hawking bananas?!``
``Let me explain that part.@k@vS12/22/181400005. This year's festival is trying something new: an auction-style event where donated items are sold off to raise funds.``
``Ahaha, there's kind of a complicated story behind it...``

``The committee wanted to collect donated items and use them as prizes for some kind of event everyone could participate in,@k@vS12/03/180300059. but lotteries and raffles are strictly off the table.``
``Long story short, our grandmother threw a fit ages ago and then couldn't back down.@k@vS12/06/180600007. So lotteries have been banned ever since.``
changeReason: Original 'she didn't really consider any specifics' completely misses the Japanese point about lotteries being banned; new correctly conveys this.

TextENGNew: ``Yeah... ahahaha...@k@vS12/03/180300061. Anyway, that's why we needed to come up with some other fun event where people could win prizes, and we settled on the auction idea.``
``An auction sounds really fun!@k@vS12/02/180200034. You might be able to snag something incredible for cheap!``
``And since everything was donated, the cost to the committee is zero.@k@vS12/04/180400076. The organizers certainly know how to turn a profit.``
``...All the profits go toward the bubbly barley tea fund for the committee's winter hot-spring trip.@k@vS12/05/180500174. That's not fair.``
changeReason: Japanese '泡麦茶' (bubbly barley tea) is a humorous euphemism for beer; original translates directly as 'beer' while new preserves the joke.

TextENGNew: ``Furude-san, you are far too young for bubbly barley tea!``
``I-I think I get the gist of it now...@k@vS12/01/180100156. ...But I've never hosted an auction before. How am I supposed to do this?``
``I'm sure you can do it, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS12/02/180200037. Plus, you get ten percent of the proceeds as spending money. Isn't that great?``
changeReason: Original frames the 10% as a hypothetical motivator; new presents it as an actual arrangement, changing the nature of the offer.

TextENGNew: ``W-Well, when you put it that way...@k@vS12/01/180100158. If I get something based on performance, then yeah, I'd be giving it my all!``
``On the other hand, there might be a dreadful penalty game waiting if you don't deliver results.``
``W-Wait a second, Satoko!@k@vS12/01/180100160. Why would I agree to something with that kind of risk?!``
changeReason: New translation truncates the last portion about being in an asking position, losing content present in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``I'm a very busy guy, you know!@k@vS12/01/180100163. Talk to my manager about my schedule first!``
``Ahahahaha!``
``So what do you say, Kei-chan?@k@vS12/03/180300065. If you need some kind of reward, I'll negotiate the terms and conditions for you. How's that sound?``
changeReason: New translation significantly restructures and truncates Mion's dialogue, losing her promise to make maximum accommodations.

TextENGNew: ``Oho!@k@vS12/01/180100165. Now that's generous!@k@vS12/01/180100166. Let me think about what to ask for...``
changeReason: Original 'That sounds interesting' vs new 'Now that's generous!' -- different reactions; Japanese 'ほー！太っ腹' means 'how generous!' matching the new version.

TextENGNew: ``We always talk about what the punishment should be, but I think this might be the first time we've ever discussed what the reward should be!``
``Now that you mention it, you're right.@k@vS12/04/180400079. We really do spend all our time fighting under the harshest conditions.``
Rena had a point.
...Fundamentally, club activities are all about escaping the penalty game -- that's the primary motivation.
Aiming for first place is a matter of principle, sure, but it's not like we play for prizes or rewards.
``...Keiichi is so flustered by this rare sweet deal that he doesn't know what to do.``
``Mii-chan, can't you suggest something?@k@vS12/02/180200040. When someone says 'anything goes,' it actually makes it harder to decide.``
``Hmm, let me think...@k@vS12/03/180300069. Ah, how about this?``
``An admission ticket to Angel Mort's Dessert Fiesta!``
``Come on, Sis, don't be stingy. Just give him like three whole years' worth.``
``Oh my!@k@vS12/04/180400081. Now that's a lavish reward!``
``If I remember right, Dessert Fiesta tickets are impossibly hard to get, and there's even a black market for them, right?@k@vS12/01/180100168. You can really arrange that?``
changeReason: New translation adds the specific name 'Dessert Fiesta' (デザートフェスタ) from the Japanese, which the original omitted.

TextENGNew: ``Of course -- but it's performance-based!@k@vS12/03/180300072. You'd better bring the house down, or no deal!``
``Don't worry.@k@vS12/02/180200042. With Keiichi-kun running it, it's guaranteed to be a blast!``
``I completely agree.@k@vS12/06/180600009. Honestly, I can't wait to see what kind of outrageous sales pitches he comes up with.``
``He could probably get people lining up for takoyaki with no octopus in it.``
``Hey, hey -- even I couldn't sell octopus-less takoyaki...``
``No, no... I bet you actually could, Kei-chan!``
``Yeah!@k@vS12/02/180200044. He'd probably call it a wholesome, healthy wheat-flour treat or something.``
``Are you guys flattering me or making fun of me? I honestly can't tell...``
``What do you say, Maebara-kun?@k@vS12/22/181400008. I'm sure you'll do a wonderful job of exciting the crowd.``
Incidentally, Chie-sensei was on the committee too.
She must have thrown her full support behind Mion's recommendation.
``...I want to see Keiichi's silver-tongue magic too.@k@vS12/05/180500177. Nipah~``
``Even banana hawkers succeed because people are so charmed by the showmanship that they can't help buying.``
``The Keiichi Maebara One-Man Show!@k@vS12/04/180400084. That sounds like such fun!``
``Hey, I haven't actually said I'd do it yet!``
``Don't worry, Keiichi-kun. You can do it.``
``Easy for you to say when it's not your problem, Rena!``
``Hau, that's not what I meant!``
The principal, who had been silently observing the exchange, finally spoke up.
``What say you, Maebara-kun?@k@vS12/00/183200002. I too believe you're up to the task.``
``Hmmmmmm...``
``As a man, won't you step up and meet everyone's expectations...?``
If they told me to do it, I could probably manage.
I'd crossed plenty of dangerous bridges in Mion's club activities by now.
All reward with no penalty game -- I'd never heard of such cushy terms before.
It would probably be fun if I took it on...
More than anything, this was the first time in my life that so many people had placed their hopes in me for something.
If I'm being honest, it made me a little happy.
...Still, a small part of me wondered if I was being sweet-talked into shouldering a bothersome role.
That thought crossed my mind briefly.
But that was the kind of thinking that belonged somewhere other than Hinamizawa.
Here in Hinamizawa, when these people smiled and talked like this, there wasn't an ounce of ulterior motive.
changeReason: Original 'The people in this village would never do anything to betray me' is a broader claim than the new translation's scene-specific observation about no ulterior motives; the Japanese describes the specific moment.

TextENGNew: They genuinely believed that if I became the emcee, the festival would come alive and become an unforgettable memory for everyone.
In the short but incredibly rich time since I'd transferred here, I already knew that about them.
``All right...@k@vS12/01/180100176. I'm a man, after all.@k@vS12/01/180100177. If you're all going to put it that way, I can't exactly turn you down!``
``Just you watch -- when I take something on, I go all out! I'll blow that auction wide open!!``
``And don't forget those Angel Mort tickets! I'll put on such a show you'll want to give me not three years' worth, but ten!!``
``That's the spirit, Kei-chan!!!``
Everyone applauded.
...How many times in my life had I received applause that was genuinely meant to celebrate me?
Never.@k This was truly the first time.
``Still, I'm surprised the committee actually agreed to nominate a kid like me.``
You'd expect the neighborhood association to be full of stubborn old folks with no patience for youngsters.
On top of that, I was a city kid who'd only just moved here -- a total outsider.
...Even granting that Mion carried considerable influence within the association, it was remarkable they'd accepted someone like me.
``Well, most of them are pretty set in their ways...@k@vS12/06/180600011. But times are gradually changing.``
``Hinamizawa won the dam conflict, and now we need to build a new era and keep growing stronger.@k@vS12/03/180300077. So welcoming young people and outsiders is essential!``
``...Keiichi is young, from the city, and well-liked on top of that -- a perfect fit.``
``Well-liked, huh?@k@vS12/01/180100182. I honestly can't figure out why so many people around here know who I am.``
``It probably has a lot to do with Mii-chan's penalty games, I think... I think.``
``I knew it!!``
````Ahahahahahahahaha!````
``Then it's settled.@k@vS12/22/181400010. I'll call your parents tonight to explain, so please mention it to them yourself as well, Maebara-kun.``
Well, I supposed parental consent would be necessary.
...My parents wouldn't object.@k If anything, I'd probably be peppered with questions about why I was nominated...
Chie-sensei and the principal praised my decisiveness, then headed back to the faculty room.
This had all happened during lunch break, incidentally.
When Chie-sensei and the principal showed up together,@k and then Mion announced she had something important to discuss, I'd been thoroughly rattled.@k My stomach was still paying the price.
``Maebara-san! I heard everything! That's amazing!``
``Being recommended by the neighborhood association is a real honor...``
``Oh, Tomita-kun, Okamura-kun.@k@vS12/01/180100185. If it's such an honor, I'd be happy to pass it along to you guys.``
``N-No way... only you could pull it off, Maebara-san!``
Looking around, the entire class was buzzing about me accepting the emcee role.
They were saying things like how this year's Watanagashi was going to be so much fun, and how they couldn't wait.
...The weight of responsibility only seemed to grow.@k Well, now that I'd accepted, I had to go all out!
``Oh, right -- I almost forgot.``
``My Granny made a mountain of ohagi.@k@vS12/03/180300081. She told me to bring them for everyone at school.``
Mion clapped her hands as if suddenly remembering, then opened the large bag she'd had with her since that morning and set a furoshiki-wrapped box on the desk.
When she lifted the lid, there inside was a generous array of ohagi, neatly lined up in rows.
``My, there are so many!@k@vS12/04/180400087. There might even be enough for the whole class!``
``Hauuu!!@k@vS12/02/180200050. So cuuute and yummy-looking!!``
``For some reason, Granny just loves making ohagi!``
``...That's because Souhei loved them so much.``
``Ah, right... I heard Gramps used to love ohagi.``
``He ate mountains of them!``
``So for your grandmother, ohagi is something precious that reminds her of your late grandfather.``
``Really?@k@vS12/06/180600014. I always thought Gramps' favorite food was canned stuff.``
``Heh heh heh!@k@vS12/03/180300085. Shion can't stand canned food, can she?``
``Hmmmm, how to put it... the canned-food smell...@k@vS12/06/180600016. That peculiar metallic odor just doesn't agree with me...``
``Oh, really?!@k@vS12/04/180400089. This coming from the person who forced me to eat pumpkin and lectured me about being a picky eater!``
``Well, you see, the truth is -- heh heh heh!!``
``W-Wait, Sis!@k@vS12/06/180600018. Telling people things they'd rather keep secret is the absolute worst!``

``I don't know what this is about, but it sounds like Mion's got some rare dirt on Shion.@k@vS12/01/180100187. This is perfect -- avenge all those times she got the better of you!@k@vS12/01/180100188. Come on, spill it!``
changeReason: Original 'Tell it to us! Go on!' vs new's encouragement to 'avenge all those times she got the better of you!' -- the Japanese mentions getting back at Shion for past grievances.

TextENGNew: The reason behind Shion's dislike of canned food was apparently hilarious -- and deeply mortifying for Shion.
The two sisters tussled for a while.
Mion's close-range knee barrage wasn't bad, but she apparently hadn't anticipated Shion countering with a captiude throw.
Shion really was a cut above...
``I'd rather explain it myself than let Sis do it.@k@vS12/06/180600020. So, there's this ornery old man named Kasai...``
changeReason: Japanese '性質の悪いオヤジ' means 'ill-natured/cantankerous old man'; original's 'bad guy' is too strong while new's 'ornery old man' is more accurate.

TextENGNew: ``And he once told me,@k@vS12/06/180600022. back when I was young and pure and innocent,@k@vS12/06/180600023. that sometimes human flesh gets mixed into canned food...``
``Ahahahaha, there's urban legends like that, aren't there?``
``Totally!@k@vS12/01/180100190. Where I'm from, people used to say the meat in beef bowls is actually dog, or that hamburgers have earthworms mixed in!``
``Th-Th-That can't be true, can it?!``
``Now I'll never be able to eat beef bowls or hamburgers again...``
``Exactly, Satoko!@k@vS12/06/180600025. I'm a victim of trauma here!``
``You have my sympathy, but it's nowhere near enough to cancel out the grudge I hold over that pumpkin full-course meal!``
``Satoko holds grudges too long.@k@vS12/05/180500182. I thought it was delicious and gobbled it right up.``
changeReason: Original 'You need to let go of that one' is generic advice; new 'Satoko holds grudges too long' correctly identifies who is being discussed and matches the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``Ahaha!@k@vS12/01/180100192. You know, all this talk...@k@vS12/01/180100193. makes me wonder if Mion's ohagi have some kind of surprise hidden in them too!``
``Oh?@k@vS12/03/180300088. These are ohagi that I brought, after all.@k@vS12/03/180300089. You can't exactly guarantee there's nothing suspicious inside, can you?``
...Mion's ohagi.
...Knowing Mion, it would be stranger if she hadn't done something to them.
...What was this feeling?@k Something... I felt like I was forgetting something...
But I had no idea what.
It must be one of those deja vu moments you get when you're tired.
Something that's happening for the first time, yet your mind tells you that you already know about it... a minor glitch in memory.
``...What's wrong, Keiichi?``
``Nothing...@k@vS12/01/180100195. I just feel like Mion's given me ohagi before...@k@vS12/01/180100196. Must be my imagination.``
``I think this is the first time, isn't it?@k@vS12/03/180300092. You must be confusing it with something else.``
``As far as I remember, this is the first time Mion-san has brought ohagi to school this year.``
``Ahhh, I get it.@k@vS12/03/180300094. Since it's this old man who brought the ohagi, you're worried there's some kind of trap, aren't you?``
``That's nothing to brag about. You should be reflecting on your usual behavior instead.``
``...Hahaha, you're right.@k@vS12/01/180100198. If Rena or someone had made these ohagi, I wouldn't feel this way at all.@k@vS12/01/180100199. Knowing it's from Mion just makes me a little wary.``
``...Huh?@k@vS12/02/180200059. Rena's never made ohagi before, though...``
...Wait, what?@k ...Right...?
Why did I just say Rena had made ohagi...?
Why would I know that Rena's ohagi would be small but carefully made...?
A confusion of memories I couldn't account for even to myself.
It would all be settled if I just admitted it was a mistake on my part.
Mion herself was saying this was the first time she'd brought ohagi.
While I sat there tilting my head in puzzlement, everyone else had already started reaching for the ohagi.
Rena took one out for me and placed it on the lid of a tupperware container.
For some reason, I found myself staring at it intently.
What was it that bothered me so much...?
Mion assumed I was worried she'd done something suspicious to it.
...But 'worried' didn't quite describe it.
How could I put it...
...It was like a sense of obligation to remember something I'd forgotten.
I had completely forgotten something I was supposed to remember,@k and all that remained was this nagging sense of duty, prodding at me -- a strange sensation.
Just a single ohagi sat before me. An ohagi that Mion had simply brought from home. Nothing more.
...A dull restlessness quietly gnawed at me.@k But I truly had no idea what it was about.
There was no way for me to remember in the first place.
...Because I 'didn't know.'
``...Keiichi, you've been staring at that ohagi for a while now. What's the matter...?``
``Oh, it's just... deja vu, I think.@k@vS12/01/180100202. You know that feeling?@k@vS12/01/180100203. Where something is happening for the first time, but you feel like you've done it before?``
``...Keiichi... you feel as though Mion has given you ohagi before...?``
``...Hahaha, it's just my imagination, I'm sure.@k@vS12/01/180100205. Mion herself just said she doesn't remember anything like that.``
``...Keiichi.@k@vS12/05/180500188. Memories that don't exist can't exist.@k@vS12/05/180500189. If you remember it, then it happened. ...I want you to believe that.``
...Rika-chan was saying something strange.
Normally, I would have brushed it off as one of her playful non sequiturs...
...But ever since I'd witnessed her 'prediction' at the toy store the other day, I'd started half-believing what the village elders said about Rika-chan's mystical powers.
changeReason: New translation more accurately conveys the Japanese, specifying the village elders' claims about Rika-chan's powers, while the original vaguely says 'curious about her special powers.'

TextENGNew: So it felt like her words carried some deeper meaning.
``Heh, coming from Rika the Great Prophet, that gives me a kind of eerie feeling.@k@vS12/01/180100207. You mean like reincarnation and past lives, that sort of thing?``
``...I don't know much about religion.``
``I just want to say this... if you know something, then it happened.@k@vS12/05/180500192. So I want you to 'remember' it.``
``Don't dismiss it as a mistake. Accept it as a real memory.@k@vS12/05/180500194. ...Do you understand what I'm trying to say...?``
``Umm... I guess...?``
I was a little taken aback -- I hadn't expected Rika-chan to talk about something so serious.
The Rika-chan I knew was a younger girl who goofed around with baby talk like 'mii~' and 'nipah~.'
But the Rika-chan in front of me now had a distinctly different air about her.
Was I supposed to play dumb and deflect with a joke...?
But Rika-chan's eyes -- more serious than I'd ever seen them -- made it perfectly clear that now was not the time for levity.
``If you say so, Rika-chan... then I guess, in some other world or something, I really did get ohagi from Mion.``
changeReason: Japanese '別の世界' means 'another world'; original mistranslates as 'previous life' while new correctly says 'in some other world.'

TextENGNew: ``...I won't laugh at you, so please tell me everything.@k@vS12/05/180500196. Where, how, and why did Mii give you ohagi?``
changeReason: Japanese asks 'どこでどうして、なぜ' (where, how, why); original only asks 'Why' while new correctly includes all three question words.

TextENGNew: ``The more specifically you ask me when, where, and how... the more it really does feel like it was all just my imagination.@k@vS12/01/180100211. It was probably just a muddled dream I had while half-asleep or something.``
``...Even if it was a dream, that's fine.@k@vS12/05/180500198. You should remember more than that.@k@vS12/05/180500199. Please, tell me.``
What had gotten into her?@k Rika-chan was being unusually persistent.
I couldn't see why this was such an interesting topic.
It didn't seem like the kind of thing Rika-chan should be getting this serious about, and I was starting to feel a gap between her intensity and mine.
changeReason: Original simplifies to just feeling uncomfortable; new accurately conveys the gap in intensity between Rika and Keiichi that the Japanese describes.

TextENGNew: But the low-grade restlessness I'd been feeling even before Rika-chan spoke to me was urging me to answer her seriously...
...Maybe Rika-chan could do fortune-telling through dream interpretation or something.
Well, might as well humor her.
She was the miko of the revered Oyashiro-sama, after all.
``Ummm...@k@vS12/01/180100213. I think I was staying home from school.@k@vS12/01/180100214. A cold, probably.``
Fragments of memory, crushed so fine you could call them powder.
...Beyond them, I could faintly see myself standing at the entrance...
...welcoming Mion... and one other person.
I think it was Rena.
``And then Mion said she'd brought ohagi as a get-well gift.@k@vS12/01/180100216. Just like today, she said her grandmother made them, I think...``
``...And then?``
``...Yeah.@k@vS12/01/180100218. And then I ate the ohagi.@k@vS12/01/180100219. After I saw the two of them off.@k@vS12/01/180100220. And inside...``
``...Ahahahahaha!@k@vS12/01/180100222. From this point on, I'm definitely just stealing from a manga.@k@vS12/01/180100223. I probably read it when I was a kid and mixed it up with a real memory.``
``...Keiichi, keep going.@k@vS12/05/180500202. You ate the ohagi, and inside it...?``
``Oh, well... when I was in elementary school, I read this scary manga where someone hid sewing needles inside a hamburger steak.``
``Ahahaha, you heard what Shion said just now, right?@k@vS12/01/180100227. When something scares you as a kid, it sticks with you as a trauma.``
My memory of the manga was quite hazy.
It was about a girl who suffered a disfiguring injury in a chance encounter and cursed the person responsible. Something like that.

Looking back now, it was a ridiculously cheap story with no plot coherence, designed solely to frighten readers.@k But for a grade-schooler like me, the oppressive art style had been terrifyingly effective.
One of the episodes featured a curse that contaminated the victim's food with all sorts of dangerous objects.
The very first scene was of a dinner hamburger steak with sewing needles hidden inside -- and the victim biting into it, going 'Gyaaah! Guehhh!'
changeReason: Japanese 'ハンバーグ' is hamburger steak (a Japanese dish), not a burger; new correctly translates as 'hamburger steak' while original says 'burger.'

TextENGNew: ...Even recalling it now sent a chill through me. It had been an incredibly grotesque and shocking scene.
It left a kind of trauma, and for a while afterward, I couldn't eat anything without meticulously checking for needles first.
So foods you bite into whole -- like rice balls or hamburgers -- were completely off-limits for a while.
...My parents eventually said something about it, and anyway, a growing boy's appetite always wins over trauma.
Just like bedwetting, that trivial little trauma eventually cured itself with time...
``Even now, if the situation seems even slightly suspicious, a part of me starts wondering if there might be a needle inside.@k@vS12/01/180100229. Of course, I know it's completely irrational.``
``When we're all sharing lunch together, it would be rude to inspect every bite.@k@vS12/01/180100231. Nobody would ever pull a dangerous prank like putting needles in food. I know that.``
``...But you're still a little scared?``
``Hmm... the fact that I still can't completely forget about it means... maybe a tiny bit of it lingers somewhere deep down.``
changeReason: Original truncates to 'Maybe I am somewhere inside my heart'; new correctly conveys the full Japanese meaning about the inability to forget suggesting something lingers deep down.

TextENGNew: ``...Then... you did find something inside Mii's ohagi...?``
``...A sewing needle.``
``Yeah.@k@vS12/01/180100234. ...It was in there.``
Inside that ohagi... as if answering my fear of needles, there was indeed a needle...
...Why was I discussing something so incoherent -- neither waking fantasy nor daydream -- with such dead seriousness?
...Mion and Rena came to visit while I was home sick, and left ohagi as a get-well gift.
...That never happened, so this was obviously a memory error.
But Rika-chan had said she wouldn't laugh, and told me to talk.@k So I talked.

``...Keiichi, does this ohagi in front of you right now frighten you? Do you feel like there might be a needle inside?@k@vS12/05/180500207. Is that why you haven't been able to touch it this whole time?``
I realized Mion and Rena were looking at me too.
...They seemed puzzled by the solemn expression on my face as I stared at the ohagi without eating it.
``Oh... Kei-chan, do you not like ohagi?@k@vS12/03/180300096. If you don't, you don't have to force yourself...``
Mion apparently assumed I disliked ohagi.
...The apologetic look on her face, as if she felt bad for pressuring me to eat something I didn't want, was practically spelled out in letters across her expression.
The instant I saw that slightly pained expression on Mion's face, it clawed at my heart.
``Sis, he obviously thinks you hid something weird inside.``
When Shion said that, I reflexively shot back.
...It was the kind of thing no one else could possibly understand.
I didn't even understand what I was saying myself.
``No -- I can say with absolute certainty that there are no needles in Mion's ohagi.``
``A-A needle?!@k@vS12/03/180300098. Why on earth would this old man put something like that in there!``

``Ahahaha... of course you wouldn't.@k@vS12/01/180100237. You're definitely not the type to just show up quietly with a get-well gift.@k@vS12/01/180100238. But you wouldn't pull a dangerous prank either.``
``You know where the line is between a joke and something serious.@k@vS12/01/180100240. So something like that would absolutely never happen.``
I grabbed the ohagi firmly in my hand.
As I brought it to my mouth, the memory of that childhood horror manga surged back one last time, stirring the urge to check the inside before biting in.
...I spoke firmly to that cowardly part of myself.
Hey -- you gutless fool!
How long are you going to let some manga you read as a kid scare you?
Mion brought these ohagi because she wanted to share them with everyone.
There are absolutely no needles in here!!
I severed ties with that petty, foolish obsession from my past and bit into the ohagi.
``...Mmm -- this is great!!``
``Of course it is!@k@vS12/03/180300100. There wasn't any needle inside, was there?!``
``Obviously not.@k@vS12/01/180100243. I'm really sorry.@k@vS12/01/180100244. I can't believe I let such a stupid trauma hold me back for so long.``
The knot in my chest dissolved, and a sense of clarity washed over me.
Then, for some reason, words I felt compelled to say rose up from deep in my chest.
...They weren't exactly appropriate for the situation, but I felt I had to say them right now.
``Hey, Mion.``
``Hm? What?``
``The ohagi was delicious.``
``Ahahahahaha -- I'm glad it was worth lugging that huge bag all the way here!``
I didn't really understand why I felt the need to tell her again that the ohagi was delicious.
But I was glad I said it to Mion.
...And with that, I was finally released from the vague restlessness that had been gnawing at me all this time.
``All right, come on!@k@vS12/06/180600029. Satoko, take a page from Kei-chan's book and chomp right into that pumpkin!@k@vS12/06/180600030. Let's go!``
changeReason: New translation substantially changes Shion's dialogue, omitting the reference to the food being soft/sweet and restructuring the encouragement differently.

TextENGNew: ``Every single thing in your lunch box turns out to be pumpkin!!@k@vS12/04/180400095. You're giving me a complex!``
``Ahahahaha!@k@vS12/02/180200061. Shii-chan is really working hard to cure Satoko-chan's picky eating!``
``She's so devoted to the cause, honestly.@k@vS12/03/180300105. Maybe she fancies herself Satoko's future sister? Heh heh heh!``
By the time I finished my ohagi, we had settled into our usual, relaxed after-meal conversation.
``...Keiichi.@k@vS12/05/180500209. Hypothetically?``
``Hm?@k@vS12/01/180100248. Hypothetically what?``
``...What if a sewing needle really did come out of your food? ...What would you do?``
``That's never going to happen.@k@vS12/01/180100250. Don't try to scare me, Rika-chan~``
``...Keiichi.@k@vS12/05/180500212. Even so... what if it really did happen...?``
As I tried to laugh it off, Rika-chan was serious again.
...What had gotten into her today...?
``You're being persistent.@k@vS12/01/180100252. That's absolutely never going to happen, and I refuse to believe otherwise.``
I had just overcome this petty trauma of mine.
So I told Rika-chan firmly.
``I understand.@k@vS12/05/180500214. ...Keiichi.@k@vS12/05/180500215. I'm sorry for being so persistent.@k@vS12/05/180500216. But I want you to remember what you just told me.``
``You're telling me not to doubt Mion, right?@k@vS12/01/180100254. Yeah, of course!@k@vS12/01/180100255. I will never doubt my friends!``
I raised my fist with conviction for her to see.
That finally seemed to satisfy Rika-chan.
She gave me a relieved smile.
